description: Summer Cut micronutrient database v2.0. Maps MacroFactor food IDs to complete nutritional profiles with bioavailability flags, amino acid profiles, glycemic index/load, and anti-nutrient interaction data.

coverage_notes.exact: USDA common foods with complete micronutrient profiles
coverage_notes.mapped_usda: Branded foods matched to equivalent USDA entries after macro comparison
coverage_notes.label_data: Branded foods with micronutrient data from manufacturer labels (typically 5-8 micronutrients)
coverage_notes.label_partial: Branded foods with very limited label data (typically only sodium, calcium, iron)
coverage_notes.label_estimated: Branded foods with micros estimated from closest USDA equivalent
coverage_notes.usda_estimated: Generic foods with micros from closest USDA profile

n_1169.name: Olive Oil
n_1169.notes: Rich in vitamin E and K

n_122407.name: Almond Breeze Almond Milk Vanilla Unsweetened
n_122407.notes: Fortified with calcium and vitamin E

uc_11463.name: Spinach Frozen Unprepared
uc_11463.notes: Outstanding source of vitamin K, vitamin A, folate, magnesium
uc_11463.interaction_notes: Very high oxalate content. Only ~5% of calcium is bioavailable (vs ~30% from dairy). Iron absorption also reduced. Cook to reduce oxalate by ~30-40%.

n_116397.name: Seaweed Salad
n_116397.notes: High sodium, good source of iodine and magnesium

n_11988.name: Popcorn, home popped, hot air popped
n_11988.notes: Excellent source of fiber and magnesium
n_11988.gi_notes: Medium GI but low GL per typical serving. Good volume snack.

n_4922.name: Apple, Fresh, With Skin
n_4922.gi_notes: Low GI. Pectin fiber slows absorption.

n_24864.name: Pam Cooking Spray Butter Flavor
n_24864.notes: Negligible per serving — mostly cooking oil base

n_2950.name: Broccoli, Raw
n_2950.notes: Outstanding source of vitamin C and K

uc_5683.name: Chicken Dark Meat Thigh Only Cooked Roasted
uc_5683.notes: Mapped from Harvest Creek branded entry. Athlete cooks own boneless skinless thighs.

bioavailability.zinc_absorption_notes: high — animal source, no phytate interference

m_13121230.name: Fully Cooked Chicken Thigh Meat (→ USDA Thigh Roasted)
m_13121230.notes: MAPPED to USDA uc_5683. Athlete cooks own boneless skinless thighs.

m_29577397.name: Egg (→ USDA Whole Egg Raw)
m_29577397.notes: MAPPED to USDA uc_1123 Egg Whole Raw. Excellent source of choline, B12, selenium.

bioavailability.zinc_absorption_notes: moderate-high — egg zinc is well absorbed

m_29569350.name: Cooked White Rice (→ USDA White Rice)
m_29569350.notes: MAPPED to USDA uc_20051. Enriched white rice.
m_29569350.gi_notes: High GI. Good for post-workout glycogen replenishment.
m_29569350.interaction_notes: Milled/enriched — phytate largely removed during processing. Good mineral bioavailability.

t_20956309.name: Cooked Steel Cut Oats (→ USDA Oatmeal)
t_20956309.notes: MAPPED to USDA cooked oatmeal, regular.
t_20956309.gi_notes: Medium GI. Steel cut lower than rolled. Sustained energy.
t_20956309.interaction_notes: Oats contain phytate. Soaking/cooking reduces it. Pair with vitamin C for iron absorption.

m_23979873.name: Cauliflower Riced Frozen (→ USDA Cauliflower)
m_23979873.notes: MAPPED to USDA cauliflower raw. Excellent source of vitamin C.

m_20488766.name: Gold Standard 100% Plant-Based Protein Vanilla
m_20488766.notes: Per serving (40g): 150cal, 24g pro. Fortified with vitamin C (100% DV), B12 (25%), iron (25%). Sodium is high (540mg/serving).

bioavailability.limiting_factor: methionine — pea protein is low in sulfur amino acids

m_29637922.name: PB Fit Powdered Peanut Butter
m_29637922.notes: Estimated micros from USDA defatted peanut flour profile. Good source of magnesium, niacin.

bioavailability.zinc_absorption_notes: low — high phytate content reduces absorption
bioavailability.limiting_factor: methionine — peanut protein is low in sulfur amino acids

m_29637922.interaction_notes: Peanut-based, significant phytate. Zinc and iron absorption reduced. Defatting process may partially reduce phytate.

m_31011707.name: Chocolate Sugar Free Instant Pudding
m_31011707.notes: Minimal micronutrients. Mostly modified starch. High sodium. Cocoa provides some iron, phosphorus, magnesium.

m_29546981.name: Beer IPA
m_29546981.notes: USDA beer average. Very limited micronutrient contribution. ~200 cal per 16oz.

o_0072220020045.name: White Bread
o_0072220020045.notes: Keto bread — very high fiber (~10g/slice). Micros estimated from label + wheat gluten/oat fiber base. Phytate content likely reduces mineral bioavailability.
o_0072220020045.gi_notes: Very low GI. High fiber keto bread — most carbs are fiber.
o_0072220020045.interaction_notes: Wheat gluten base contains phytate. Reduces iron and zinc absorption by ~30-50%. Mitigated when eaten with vitamin C or animal protein.

m_32055745.name: Keto English Muffins
m_32055745.notes: Keto muffin — very high fiber. Micros estimated from label + wheat gluten base.
m_32055745.gi_notes: Very low GI. High fiber keto product.
m_32055745.interaction_notes: Same phytate concern as keto bread. Pair with vitamin C source.

o_0850000429116.name: Cookies & Cream Protein Bar
o_0850000429116.notes: Protein bar. Very limited label data.

o_0888849005994.name: Protein Cookie Chocolate Chip
o_0888849005994.notes: Protein cookie. High fat for a protein product.

o_0888849013555.name: Tortilla Style Protein Chips Hot & Spicy
o_0888849013555.notes: High protein tortilla chips. High sodium.

o_0810035970601.name: Keto Brown Sugar Poptart
o_0810035970601.notes: Keto pastry. Very limited micro data.

o_0810035972636.name: Protein Donuts Vanilla Glazed
o_0810035972636.notes: Protein donut. Very limited micro data.

o_0047495013054.name: Fig Bar Raspberry
o_0047495013054.notes: Fig bar. Whole wheat, mostly carbs.

o_0038000359217.name: Nutri Grain Strawberry Breakfast Bar
o_0038000359217.notes: Breakfast bar. Heavily fortified with B vitamins, iron, folate.

o_0030000169247.name: Cheddar Flavored Rice Crisps
o_0030000169247.notes: Rice snack. Very limited micro data.

o_4901326012073.name: Squid Pea Snack
o_4901326012073.notes: Japanese snack. Label only shows sodium.

o_4901326011960.name: 脆豆 (Crispy Beans)
o_4901326011960.notes: Japanese snack. Label only shows sodium.

o_4901326006377.name: Shrimp Hot Chili Wheat Chips
o_4901326006377.notes: Japanese snack. Label only shows sodium.

o_0857900005198.name: Drizzling
o_0857900005198.notes: Rice cake drizzle snack. Minimal micros.

o_0036632037428.name: Greek Banana Cream
o_0036632037428.notes: Light Greek yogurt. Calcium and B12 from dairy. Estimated from USDA nonfat Greek yogurt.

m_30144617.name: Iced Hazelnut Latte Tall
m_30144617.notes: Coffee drink. Calcium from milk. One-off entry.

o_0051000288110.name: Creamy Chicken Cajun Style Alfredo
o_0051000288110.notes: Canned soup. High sodium. One-off entry.

m_1466886.name: Garlic Fries
m_1466886.notes: Estimated from USDA frozen french fries profile. Approximate.

m_16576150.name: Miracle Whip Light Dressing
m_16576150.notes: Estimated from USDA light mayonnaise profile. Mostly fat + sodium. Vitamin E and K from oil base.

10434536.name: Sriracha Hot Chilli Sauce
10434536.notes: Mapped to USDA 171186 Sauce, sriracha, hot chile. Good source of vitamin A, E, C from peppers.

t_16160630.name: Bone Suckin' Sauce
t_16160630.notes: BBQ sauce. Mostly sugar and sodium. Estimated from USDA BBQ sauce profile.

m_4059441.name: Triple Berry Frozen Fruit Blend
m_4059441.notes: Estimated from USDA mixed berries frozen. Good source of vitamin C, manganese.
m_4059441.gi_notes: Low GI. Berries are among the lowest-GI fruits.

m_17029672.name: Yellowfin Tuna Sashimi
m_17029672.notes: USDA yellowfin tuna raw. Excellent source of niacin, B6, B12, selenium.

m_19488800.name: Sashimi Salmon
m_19488800.notes: USDA Atlantic salmon raw. Outstanding source of B12, D, selenium, omega-3.

m_614430.name: Hamachi Young Yellowtail
m_614430.notes: Estimated from USDA mixed yellowtail data. Good source of B12, selenium.

n_3151.name: Sweet Potato, Baked in Skin
n_3151.notes: USDA 168483 baked in skin. Outstanding vitamin A (961mcg = 107% DV). Excellent potassium source. GI ~65 baked.
n_3151.gi_notes: Medium GI. Lower when boiled vs baked.

o_08667031.name: Pink Salmon, Canned, Solids with Bone and Liquid
o_08667031.notes: USDA 15090. Bones provide exceptional calcium (283mg). Outstanding B12, D, selenium, omega-3. One of the most nutrient-dense proteins in the diet.

m_646459.name: Low Sugar Strawberry Preserves
m_646459.notes: Fruit preserves. Mostly sugar. Some vitamin C from strawberries. Minimal micro contribution.

m_30710485.name: Mixed Vegetables, Frozen, Unprepared
m_30710485.notes: USDA 11583 frozen mixed vegetables. Good vitamin A (from carrots). Decent fiber.

o_0888849005406.name: Quest Hero Protein Bar Crispy Chocolate Caramel Pecan
o_0888849005406.notes: Protein bar. High fiber from soluble corn fiber. Calcium from milk protein. Limited micro data.

o_0788434115681.name: Reese's Peanut Butter Lovers Protein Bar
o_0788434115681.notes: Protein bar. Peanut butter base adds some magnesium. Limited micro data.

o_0644225727139.name: Protein Energy Bar
o_0644225727139.notes: Wafer-style protein bar. High fat from cream filling. Limited micro data.

n_120142.name: Think! High Protein Bar Brownie Crunch
n_120142.notes: USDA entry. Fortified with vitamins and minerals. One of the more nutrient-dense protein bars.

o_0840229305506.name: Built Puff Cookies N Cream
o_0840229305506.notes: Marshmallow-style protein bar. High protein density. Limited micro data.

creatine_monohydrate.name: Creatine Monohydrate
creatine_monohydrate.notes: 15g is a maintenance/loading dose. Standard maintenance is 3-5g/day. 15g is fine short-term but no additional benefit over 5g for maintenance after muscles are saturated (takes ~7 days at 15-20g). Consider tapering to 5g/day after 2 weeks.

magnesium_glycinate.name: Magnesium Glycinate
magnesium_glycinate.notes: Chelated glycinate form — high bioavailability. 160mg is 38% of RDA (420mg for adult males). Good for sleep quality. Rhonda would approve.

trace_minerals.name: Only Trace Minerals
trace_minerals.notes: Zinc 20mg (182% RDA), Copper 2mg (222% RDA), Manganese 2mg (87% RDA), Chromium 400mcg, Molybdenum 250mcg, Boron 3mg, Vanadium 750mcg. All in bioavailable chelated forms.

fish_oil.name: Triple Strength Omega-3 Fish Oil
fish_oil.notes: 2 softgels = 2x 1040mg omega-3 per softgel. Total 1380mg EPA + 520mg DHA. Excellent dose — well above the 250-500mg EPA+DHA minimum. Rhonda would be thrilled.

vitamin_d3.name: Vitamin D3 5000 IU
vitamin_d3.notes: 125mcg (5000 IU). RDA is 15mcg (600 IU), tolerable upper limit is 100mcg (4000 IU). You're above the UL but 5000 IU is widely used and generally safe for adults. Ideal for Seattle latitude where sun exposure is limited.

meal_timing_schema.description: Schema for temporal nutrition analysis. Food log entries from MacroFactor include hour/minute fields. This schema defines how to analyze peri-workout nutrition from those timestamps.

pre_workout.label: Pre-Workout
pre_workout.notes: Target 30-50g carbs, moderate protein. GI matters — medium-high GI preferred for rapid availability.

peri_workout.label: Intra-Workout
peri_workout.notes: Only relevant for sessions >90 min. Not applicable for typical lifting.

post_workout.label: Post-Workout
post_workout.notes: Target 30-50g protein with >2.5g leucine. High GI carbs for glycogen replenishment. The 'anabolic window' is wider than bro-science suggests — 2 hours is fine.

pre_sleep.label: Pre-Sleep
pre_sleep.notes: Casein-type slow protein preferred. Magnesium glycinate supports sleep quality. Avoid large volumes.

breakfast.label: Breakfast
breakfast.notes: High protein breakfast improves satiety throughout the day during a deficit.

meal_timing_schema.analysis_approach: Cross-reference food log entry hour/minute with training session timestamps. Map each entry to the nearest meal window. Calculate per-window macro and leucine totals. Flag if post-workout window has <25g protein or <2g leucine.

v2.0: Filled sparse micro gaps for 28 foods using USDA reference data
Added 6 missing foods from food logs (sweet potato, pink salmon, mixed veg, preserves, 2 protein bars)
Added bioavailability fields: iron_type (heme/non-heme), absorption_pct, protein_digestibility_score
Added amino acid profiles (EAAs including leucine) for all protein sources
Added glycemic_index and glycemic_load for all carb sources
Added antinutrient interaction data (phytate, oxalate) with absorption impact notes
Added meal_timing_schema for peri-workout nutrition analysis
Upgraded supplement stack notes with creatine taper recommendation
